steps.title: Compare the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1: McFerrin considers background research "its own reward." Sentence 2: she admits finding research "just as fascinating and engaging as exploring a location in person." The second sentence intensifies and confirms the first — same idea pushed further.

steps.title: Decide what the transition must do
steps.content: The blank needs a word that emphasizes or strengthens the prior claim, not contrasts with it, parallels a different subject, or simply tacks on extra info.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'By contrast,': signals opposition, but sentence 2 reinforces sentence 1 rather than opposing it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Likewise,': signals a parallel claim about a different subject, but both sentences are about the same person and the same research. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Besides,': signals an additional, separate point, but sentence 2 restates and intensifies sentence 1 rather than adding a new point. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In fact,': signals that what follows strengthens or confirms the prior claim, matching the intensification from "its own reward" to "just as fascinating...as exploring in person." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
